current_expansion_flow: Optional query retry hook: _classify_initial_failure_reason -> QueryIntentParser.parse(previous_queries, failure_reason) -> _discovery
Loop per target: dedupe via explored_location_keys -> inspect_packet(read_snippet only) -> analyzer.analyze -> decision_mapper
Action branch: expand -> GraphExpander.expand(max_nodes,max_depth) OR refine -> _run_discovery_traced + _enqueue_ranked OR stop
Result: ExplorationResult(metadata includes completion_status, termination_reason, explored_files, explored_symbols)

entry_points.role: Primary ACT path entry into exploration; gates planner on exploration completion
Routes to v2 engine when ENABLE_EXPLORATION_ENGINE_V2 is true
Main loop entry; owns expansion/refinement control loop

graph_traversal_path.primary: GraphExpander.expand -> fetch_graph(query=symbol, top_k=max_nodes) -> map rows to callers/callees/related targets
graph_traversal_path.fallback: If graph empty: dispatcher SEARCH for '<symbol> callers callees definition' -> _extract_targets
graph_traversal_path.note: max_depth is carried in result metadata but traversal logic is effectively 1-hop in current adapter

cases.assessment: Refine path is reachable; cooldown then forces expand when eligible.
Sufficient stop behavior observed via terminal reason.

validated_hypotheses.hypothesis: No structured expansion strategy (graph vs search vs refine unclear)
validated_hypotheses.evidence: Expansion/refinement strategy is explicit: _should_expand/_should_refine + GraphExpander.expand + discovery refine loop. Distinct action branches are coded and live-observed.

validated_hypotheses.hypothesis: Expansion not grounded in analyzer signals (gaps/findings ignored)
validated_hypotheses.evidence: Gap-driven bridge exists (_apply_gap_driven_decision maps knowledge_gaps to needs and forces expand), but expansion target generation still does not consume a structured gap object; GraphExpander receives only symbol/file.
There is local heuristic prioritization (_target_priority_score: new symbol/file + expansion source bonus). Missing: analyzer-gap-weighted prioritization and graph-edge relevance scoring.

validated_hypotheses.hypothesis: Risk of uncontrolled expansion (no depth/budget limits)
validated_hypotheses.evidence: Hard controls exist in config and loop: EXPLORATION_MAX_STEPS, EXPLORATION_MAX_BACKTRACKS, EXPLORATION_EXPAND_MAX_NODES, EXPLORATION_EXPAND_MAX_DEPTH, EXPLORATION_STAGNATION_STEPS, EXPLORATION_UTILITY_NO_IMPROVEMENT_STREAK.

Core duplicate prevention exists via explored_location_keys, pending queue dedupe, and seen_relation_edges. Live runs still report dedupe_skips in all cases (work is generated then filtered, indicating upstream redundancy remains).

Stopping criteria are explicit and multi-layered: _should_stop_pre/_should_stop + utility stop + no_relevant_candidate + pending_exhausted + stalled + max_steps. Live utility_stop case terminated by no_improvement_streak.
There is utility tracking with improvement signature and no_improvement_streak. Missing: per-expansion-edge usefulness score used to rank/accept/reject future expansions.

depth_tracking.evidence: Config carries EXPLORATION_EXPAND_MAX_DEPTH; GraphExpander accepts max_depth and echoes it, but adapter logic is not true multi-depth traversal.

determinism_vs_llm.evidence: Control state machine is deterministic; intent parsing, candidate selection/scoping, and analyzer are LLM-driven.

expansion_input.details: Analyzer output affects decision.needs/next_action; GraphExpander itself receives (symbol, file_path, state, max_nodes, max_depth) without structured gap payload.

expansion_output.details: GraphExpander returns list[ExplorationTarget] + ExecutionResult; refine path regenerates discovery queries via QueryIntentParser retry/refine.

missing_signals_or_schema_ambiguities: No explicit typed schema for expansion requests (e.g., reason=callers|callees|definition with priority and budget context).
No explicit per-target provenance score in ExplorationTarget (only source string).
Gap semantics are string-based; no typed gap taxonomy/weight for deterministic prioritization.

control_gaps: GraphExpander depth parameter is not fully realized as true multi-hop controlled traversal.
Expansion selection is weakly tied to analyzer gaps beyond keyword-to-needs mapping.
No explicit budget ledger per strategy mode (graph/search/refine) to prevent mode starvation or overuse.
Usefulness is tracked globally (streak/signature) but not attached to specific expansion edges/targets.
Fallback search expansion can blur graph-vs-search intent and reduce explainability.

failure_modes.evidence: All live cases reported dedupe_skips > 0, indicating duplicate candidate generation before queue filters.
Frequent termination at primary_symbol_sufficient after 1 iteration can end exploration before broader dependency coverage.
Graph fallback SEARCH query is generic ('callers callees definition') and not gap-specific, risking off-target nodes.
max_depth is configured but current expander path is effectively shallow.

structured_expansion_modes.semantic_search_mode: Use when analyzer indicates concept-level gaps or graph confidence low.
structured_expansion_modes.refinement_mode: Use when wrong_target/low relevance with explicit failure reason classes.

prioritization_strategy.core: Score candidates by analyzer gap alignment, novelty (unseen file/symbol), graph confidence, and recency penalty.

budget_controls.max_depth: Enforce true hop depth in graph traversal, not metadata-only.
budget_controls.max_nodes: Per-iteration and global node budgets, strategy-specific caps.
budget_controls.max_iterations: Keep global EXPLORATION_MAX_STEPS; add per-mode step ceilings.

stopping_criteria.primary: analyzer sufficient + required gap classes resolved
stopping_criteria.secondary: diminishing returns using per-edge usefulness over rolling window
stopping_criteria.guardrail: hard stop on budget exhaustion or repeated low-utility expansions

state_tracking.visited_nodes: Keep explored_location_keys and add structured visit metadata (first_seen_step,last_score,last_reason).
state_tracking.explored_paths: Track path signatures (anchor->edge->target) with utility outcomes.

implementation_plan: Define typed contracts: ExpansionRequest, ExpansionTargetScored, GapSignal (no prompt changes).
Refactor GraphExpander to execute true bounded multi-hop traversal honoring depth budget.
Introduce deterministic expansion planner that selects mode (graph/search/refine) from analyzer + state signals.
Add per-target usefulness scoring and persist in ExplorationState path ledger.
Add strategy-level budget accounting and expose in metadata for observability.
Strengthen completion gate: require either resolved critical gaps or explicit budget exhaustion rationale.
Extend live harness to emit path-level utility metrics and mode budget consumption for regression checks.

final_assessment.is_robust_or_naive: moderately robust but still naive in expansion semantics
final_assessment.why: Control guards and stopping logic are strong; expansion targeting and usefulness attribution remain coarse and string-driven.
